coveredRequirements: Individual slash commands registered, not single dispatcher (req 16)
/git-checkout without arguments shows interactive branch picker (req 5, 25)
/git-checkout on dirty tree prompts stash, auto-stash/unstash (req 18, 26)
/git-create-branch creates and checks out new branch (req 6)
/git-remote-main prompts to stash on dirty tree (req 26)
/git-remote-main when already on main skips checkout (req 32)
/git-commit stages all and commits with message (req 8, 21)
/git-commit with no changes notifies nothing to commit (req 33)
/git-pr creates PR via gh pr create against main (req 9, 20)
/git-pr detects existing PR and updates instead (req 9, 28)
/git-pr on main refuses with helpful message (req 34)
Stash pop conflict notifies error and leaves stash intact (req 36)
ambiguousRequirements: Req 22: Utility functions (trimOutput, shQuote, detectRepoRoot, ensureGhAuthenticated) are inlined — cannot test isolation without knowing export structure; tested indirectly via behavior
Req 24: 'runs from a pi session without dropping to shell' is architectural; tested indirectly by verifying commands use ctx.exec rather than child_process directly
Req 23: Exact diff truncation threshold is unspecified; test only asserts output is shorter than raw diff
iterations.summary: The test suite provides comprehensive behavioral coverage of all plan requirements, edge cases, and acceptance criteria. Tests are well-structured with realistic mocks, strong behavioral assertions, and clear mapping to requirements. Minor issues exist but none are blocking.

lastSummary: The test suite provides comprehensive behavioral coverage of all plan requirements, edge cases, and acceptance criteria. Tests are well-structured with realistic mocks, strong behavioral assertions, and clear mapping to requirements. Minor issues exist but none are blocking.

lastFindings.title: Command discovery heuristics may not match actual extension pattern
lastFindings.details: The tests use two discovery mechanisms (collectRegisteredCommands and findCommand) to handle unknown extension shape. The plan clearly states the extension follows worktree.ts pattern using pi.registerCommand(), but the collectRegisteredCommands helper passes a mockPi object and tries calling the extension as a function or via init/setup/default methods. If the actual extension uses a different registration pattern (e.g., the extension function receives a different argument shape), all tests will fail at the handler lookup step rather than at the behavioral assertion.
lastFindings.fix: This is acceptable for TDD since the tests define the expected contract. No change needed unless the actual worktree.ts pattern is significantly different from what's assumed. Consider adding a comment noting the expected registration pattern based on worktree.ts.
lastFindings.requirement: Extension loads via the existing discovery
evidence: collectRegisteredCommands function tries ext as function, ext.init, ext.setup, ext.default

lastFindings.title: git-commit interactive prompt test doesn't verify the prompted message is used in commit
lastFindings.details: The '/git-commit without a message argument prompts for one interactively' test verifies that ctx.ui.input() is called but does not assert that the returned message ('prompted message') actually appears in the git commit command.
lastFindings.fix: Add an assertion checking that the git commit exec call includes the prompted message string: assert.ok(ctx.execCalls.find(c => c.command.includes('git commit') && c.command.includes('prompted message')), 'must use the interactively provided message in the commit')
evidence: ctx.ui.input = async (_prompt: string) => { inputCalled = true; return 'prompted message'; };

lastFindings.title: Test uses node:test but generator metadata says vitest
lastFindings.details: Generator metadata specifies framework as 'vitest' but the actual generated tests use 'node:test' and 'node:assert/strict'. The generator notes say this matches local conventions. This is fine for execution but creates a metadata inconsistency.
lastFindings.fix: No code change needed — the tests correctly follow the repository's existing test conventions (node:test). The metadata should ideally be corrected but this doesn't affect test quality.

lastFindings.title: Missing major behavioral requirement
lastFindings.details: No generated coverage entry clearly maps to: `/git-status` — show `git status --short`, notify result
lastFindings.fix: Add an explicit behavioral test for this requirement or explain why it cannot be tested realistically.
